title: Vivi’s Mission Remains Urgent
description: On pages 13–16, this part focuses on vivi’s mission remains urgent, keeping the scene centered on the conflict, choices, and stakes of the Alabasta arc.
scene: Vivi’s Mission Remains Urgent. Baroque Works’ senior agents are summoned while the Straw Hats and Vivi continue moving through the desert toward the heart of Alabasta’s crisis. This section presents the immediate scene beat before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: And that pitfall is a drought in lands leeward!

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: Can you understand what I'm getting at?

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: Dance Powder is something that artificially stimulates premature rain clouds,

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: Dance powder

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: To make them rain much earlier then they should, thereby...

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: Ohhh, I get it! The rain clouds that normally drift leewards would now rain much sooner, so it'd be like stealing rain from your neighbouring countries!

dialogue.speaker: Baroque Works agent
dialogue.text: Exactly... Once people realized that,

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 2 Bon Clay
dialogue.text: Wars were started and countless of lives have been lost...

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: And that's why the World Government has officially banned producing or owning Dance Powder.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 1
dialogue.text: So it's a powder that can be both a blessing or a tragedy, depending on how you use it...

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: Once that huge amount of dance powder was uncovered at the port of Nanohana,

dialogue.speaker: Baroque Works agent
dialogue.text: The entire nation finally realized,

dialogue.speaker: Baroque Works agent
dialogue.text: The truth behind the capital's abnormally abundant rainfall...

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: I guess it's only expected,

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: That the people would suspect the king, after an incident like that...

dialogue.speaker: Miss Doublefinger
dialogue.text: Holy crap, VIVI! Your dad is evil!

dialogue.speaker: Miss Doublefinger
dialogue.text: You idiot! He was obvi- Ously set-up!

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: There's no way he'd actually do such a thing!

dialogue.speaker: Baroque Works agent
dialogue.text: Thinking about it now, Crocoodile's plans must have already been well under way back then...

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: My innocent father was completely dumbfounded by the incident,

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: But the fact remained that a large amount of Dance Powder had been somehow delivered to the Royal Palace.

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: It was all a trap laid out by Crocodile... And just as he intended, a rebellion arose!

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: The start of a bloody conflict between the angry citizens who had lost their homes to the drought, and the innocent government...!

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: The country's peace, its trust in the King, the rain,

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: People's homes and even their lives...

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: All of it were stripped away because of one man!

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: Because of Croco- Dile!

dialogue.speaker: Toto
dialogue.text: What gives him the right to do such a thing!?

dialogue.speaker: Koza
dialogue.text: I will...

dialogue.speaker: Koza
dialogue.text: Never forgive that man!

dialogue.speaker: Baroque Works agent
dialogue.text: Hmph... Bunch of kids...

summary: This part develops vivi’s mission remains urgent and pushes the Alabasta conflict closer to its next decisive turn.
